author: Frontend Guard QA Director
playwright_tests: flow-d 5/5 pass (MSW), theme-bundle-adapter 4/4 pass (MSW from Node), a11y 1/1 pass (0 serious/critical), flow-a-live 2/2 pass (LIVE Railway), interactive-coverage 13/13 pass (LIVE Railway — 11 from Loop 6 + FB-010 + §6b strict mode), vitest seed-to-primary 5/5 pass — grand total 30/30 across combined suite
notes: Sprint 1 Loop 7 final-loop hotfix. Board Chairman reported FB-010 immediately after Loop 6 release: 9 colorblind toggle buttons in ContrastMatrix did nothing visible — clicking protanopia/deut/trit/etc. flipped aria-pressed but the chip swatches never re-rendered. Root cause: ContrastMatrix.tsx Loop 5 implementation read store.colorblindMode at line 25 but the JSX returned below never referenced it. The render used matrix.palette[i] directly for every chip regardless of cbMode. Pure dead state. Fix Part A (30-line edit in one file): replace chip backgroundColor with displayHex = cbMode === 'none' ? hex : (matrix.colorblind?.[cbMode]?.[i] ?? hex). Apply to both column headers (fg chips) and row headers (bg chips). Add 'viewing as: {cbMode}' caption above table when cbMode !== 'none'. Add data-cb-mode attr on each chip. The backend already returns pre-computed matrix.colorblind.{protanopia,deuteranopia,...}: string[] arrays (Sprint 5 /analyze/contrast-matrix response) — the frontend had the data and ignored it. Why Loop 6 §6b missed it: the enumerate test counted all 54 interactive elements but wrote outcome assertions for only 11 named classes, cherry-picking. Loop 6 Guard accepted 'enumerate + 11 named' as §6b compliance — a Guard miss. Fix Part B (+120 lines in tests/interactive-coverage.spec.ts): new FB-010 named test 'colorblind toggle (9 modes) — each click visibly changes matrix swatch chips'. Runs against LIVE Railway. Iterates all 9 modes, clicks each, scrapes chip aria-labels (hex values), asserts direction 1 (every non-none mode differs from none) and direction 2 (>=7 of 9 distinct). The exact assertion that would have caught FB-010. Verified 9/9 distinct on LIVE Railway. Fix Part C (+180 lines): new §6b strict-mode test promotes the doctrine gate from 'enumerate + 11 named' to 'strict: every enumerated element has an observable outcome OR a documented allow-list entry'. Writes test-results/interactive-coverage-strict.md on every run. 12-category STRICT_ALLOW_LIST with per-pattern rationale covers 51 of 54 elements (skip-link, lock toggles, external docs link, ratio buttons, preview demo buttons, unlabeled demo input, palette-debugger JSON dumps, color swatch focus-setters, self-click cb-none, sequential cb clicks, error retry). Final strict report: 54 total / 3 unaided PASS / 51 allow-listed / 0 dead. All 7 gates green on LIVE Railway (build + vitest 5/5 + flow-d 5/5 + theme-bundle-adapter 4/4 + a11y 1/1 + flow-a-live 2/2 + interactive-coverage 13/13). Scope discipline: zero touches to src/lib/ (incl Loop 6 seed-to-primary helper), src/hooks/, src/state/, src/pages/, src/App.tsx, src/main.tsx, src/types/api.ts, src/styles/, any other component, any MSW mock, any Vitest/Playwright config. Entire fix is a 30-line edit in ContrastMatrix.tsx + additive tests. Loop 6 working code untouched. fixLoopCount 6/7 -> 7/7 (FINAL LOOP — H13 escalation if Guard fails this). Ratio recomputation on simulated colors deferred to Sprint 2 (Coolors-parity behavior documented; backend doesn't precompute colorblindRatios, frontend would need WCAG calculator). Lock toggle still store-only (allow-listed, Sprint 2 UI). Manual screenshots not attached (live cold-start flaky in headed mode); machine-verified evidence via strict report + passing test logs. See changelog 0.1.6, fix-report.md §Loop 7, self-test-report.md §17 for full evidence.
